hypothesis: Correcting the aromatic valency bug and using chemically accurate valency representations (including a Kekulized dataset) when training 3D molecular generative models produces generated molecules with more chemically valid and geometrically accurate structures—exhibiting lower bond, angle, and torsion deviations and lower GFN2-xTB relaxation energies—than models trained on the original flawed dataset, and GFN2-xTB-based evaluation more reliably reflects true geometry quality than MMFF94 which yields artificially large relaxation energies.
